title: PRISM
text:
PRISM is a comprehensive machine learning toolkit designed to advance auditory scene analysis (Ecological Momentary Assessment) for hearing devices. This repository contains the code, models, and tools developed for the “Australian Future Hearing Initiative”.
PRISM might stand for:
Modern hearing aids often struggle to adapt to rapidly changing sound environments—like moving from a quiet room to a busy street or a noisy restaurant. This technology bridges the gap between raw audio processing and intelligent environmental awareness, leading to a more seamless and personalised hearing experience for users.

title: PRISM
text:
PRISM
Modern hearing aids often struggle to adapt to rapidly changing sound environments—like moving from a quiet room to a busy street or a noisy restaurant. This technology bridges the gap between raw audio processing and intelligent environmental awareness, leading to a more seamless and personalised hearing experience for users.

What is “Ecological Momentary Assessment” (EMA)?
EMA is used to “catch you in the moment.” When you interact with the PRISM app, we capture a snapshot of your real-world environment. This includes: - Audio: A short 30-second clip of the sound around you. - Context: Your rating of how hard it was to hear speech.
This data trains a Machine Learning models to automatically recognize difficult listening situations and adjust hearing aids in real-time.


Key Resources

Paper: A dataset and model for auditory scene recognition for hearing devices: AHEAD-DS and OpenYAMNet (arXiv:2508.10360)
Datasets:

AHEAD-DS (HuggingFace): The primary dataset for training and benchmarking.
AHEAD-DS Unmixed (HuggingFace): Component audio files before mixing, useful for custom augmentation strategies.

Models:

OpenYAMNet Models (HuggingFace): Pre-trained models ready for inference or fine-tuning.

title: PRISM
text:
Capabilities

Benchmark New Models: Use the standardized ahead_ds split to compare your model’s performance against the OpenYAMNet baseline.
Fine-Tuning: Leverage pre-trained YAMNet+ weights to adapt the model to new acoustic domains with minimal data.
Reproducibility: Full scripts for dataset generation (prism-ml-dataset-ahead-ds) and model training (prism-ml-yamnetp-tune) are provided.

title: PRISM
text:
Citation
If you use this code, model, or dataset in your research, please cite:
@misc{zhong2026datasetmodelauditoryscene,
      title={A dataset and model for auditory scene recognition for hearing devices: AHEAD-DS and OpenYAMNet}, 
      author={Henry Zhong and Jörg M. Buchholz and Julian Maclaren and Simon Carlile and Richard Lyon},
      year={2026},
      eprint={2508.10360},
      archivePrefix={arXiv},
      primaryClass={cs.SD},
      url={https://arxiv.org/abs/2508.10360}, 
}

title: Developer Guide
text: The PRISM Ecosystem consists of a backend server built with FastAPI and a web frontend built with Flutter. Both rely on Firebase for authentication and some database storage. This guide provides instructions on how to set up the PRISM system locally for development or deployment.

title: Developer Guide
text: The PRISM Ecosystem consists of a backend server built with FastAPI and a web frontend built with Flutter. Both rely on Firebase for authentication and some database storage. This guide provides instructions on how to set up the PRISM system locally for development or deployment.

title: Developer Guide
text:
Unified Setup via Docker
The easiest way to install and run both the PRISM Backend and Frontend is via the prism-setup repository, which uses Docker Compose.

1. Prerequisites
Ensure the following are installed and running: * Docker Desktop * Git * A Firebase project with paid usage enabled (Blaze plan). * A Mailgun account (or a Gmail account with 2-Step Verification and App Password) for sending emails to participants.


2. Clone the Repository
git clone https://github.com/Australian-Future-Hearing-Initiative/prism-setup.git
cd prism-setup


3. Configure Keys and Secrets
Create a .env file in the prism-setup directory:

JWT Secret: Generate a 32-character hex string (e.g. openssl rand -hex 32) and set JWT_SECRET=xxxx.
Email Settings:

Mailgun: Add MAILGUN_API_KEY and MAILGUN_AUTHORIZED_DOMAIN.
Gmail: Enable an App Password, and define MAIL_USERNAME, MAIL_FROM, MAIL_PASSWORD, MAIL_SERVER=smtp.gmail.com, MAIL_PORT=587, MAIL_STARTTLS=True, etc.

Firebase Web API Key: Find your Web API Key in your Firebase Console under “Project settings → General” and set FIREBASE_API_KEY=xxxxx.
Firebase Service Account Key: Download an Admin SDK Private Key as a JSON file, rename it serviceAccountKey.json, place it in the prism-setup directory, and set FIREBASE_KEY_PATH=./serviceAccountKey.json.



4. Install and Run
Run the installer script to prepare Dockerfiles and clone necessary repositories:
bash install.sh
Launch both frontend and backend using Docker Compose:
docker compose up --build

Frontend Dashboard: http://localhost:3000
Backend API: http://localhost:8000 (Swagger docs at /docs)

title: Developer Guide
text:
Manual Backend Setup
If you wish to run the FastAPI backend independently without Docker:

1. Python Environment
We recommend using uv for managing dependencies and virtual environments.
uv venv
source .venv/bin/activate
uv sync


2. Environment Variables
Add .env to the root of the backend directory containing: * FIREBASE_KEY_PATH=./serviceAccountKey.json * FIREBASE_API_KEY * JWT_SECRET * Email variables (MAILGUN_* or MAIL_*)


3. Run the Development Server
uv run -- uvicorn app.main:app --reload
View the Swagger UI documentation at http://127.0.0.1:8000/docs.

title: Developer Guide
text:
Manual Frontend Setup
The frontend is a Flutter web application.

1. Setup Environment Variables
Create a .env file in the root of the prism-flutter-web-frontend directory to link your backend:
API_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8000


2. Flutter Version Management (FVM)
The project utilizes FVM to ensure version consistency. Install it via dart pub global activate fvm or brew install fvm.


3. Run the App
fvm flutter channel stable
fvm flutter upgrade
fvm flutter pub get
fvm flutter run -d chrome
Run tests with flutter test or integration tests via flutter drive.

title: User Guide
text:
PRISM is a comprehensive machine learning toolkit designed to advance auditory scene analysis (Ecological Momentary Assessment-EMA) for hearing devices. This repository contains the code, models, and tools developed for the “Australian Future Hearing Initiative”.
PRISM helps researchers collect real-world data (“catch you in the moment”) through the smartphone application. When a participant interacts with the PRISM app, we capture a snapshot of your real-world environment. This includes: - Audio: A short 30-second clip of the sound around you. - Context: Your rating of how hard it was to hear speech.
This data trains a Machine Learning models to automatically recognize difficult listening situations and adjust hearing aids in real-time.
The PRISM web frontend serves as the interface for data collection and study management for Researchers conducting “experiments” within the PRISM ecosystem. Researchers use this application to design experiments, manage study cohorts, and deploy tasks to participants. It is the control center for your data collection needs.

title: User Guide
text:
PRISM is a comprehensive machine learning toolkit designed to advance auditory scene analysis (Ecological Momentary Assessment-EMA) for hearing devices. This repository contains the code, models, and tools developed for the “Australian Future Hearing Initiative”.

When a participant interacts with the PRISM app, we capture a snapshot of your real-world environment. This includes: - Audio: A short 30-second clip of the sound around you. - Context: Your rating of how hard it was to hear speech.
This data trains a Machine Learning models to automatically recognize difficult listening situations and adjust hearing aids in real-time.
The PRISM web frontend serves as the interface for data collection and study management for Researchers conducting “experiments” within the PRISM ecosystem. Researchers use this application to design experiments, manage study cohorts, and deploy tasks to participants. It is the control center for your data collection needs.
